title: Exercises: Routing and SWAP Insertion
description: Practice implementing routing algorithms for quantum circuits

exercises.title: Shortest Path Finding
exercises.description: Implement BFS to find the shortest path between two qubits on a topology graph.
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import deque

def shortest_path(edges, start, end):
    """
    Find shortest path using BFS.
    
    Args:
        edges: Set of (q1, q2) tuples for connected qubits
        start, end: Qubit indices
    Returns:
        List of qubits forming path, or [] if no path
    """
    if start == end:
        return [start]
    
    # Build adjacency list
    neighbors = {}
    for q1, q2 in edges:
        neighbors.setdefault(q1, []).append(q2)
        neighbors.setdefault(q2, []).append(q1)
    
    # TODO: BFS to find path
    pass

# Test
edges = {(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3)}
print(shortest_path(edges, 0, 3))  # [0, 1, 2, 3]
hints: Track path along with each node
Mark visited when adding to queue

exercises.title: SWAP Application

# Test

exercises.title: Basic Routing
exercises.description: Implement basic routing that inserts SWAPs for non-adjacent gates.
exercises.starterCode:
def basic_route(gate, mapping, edges):
    """
    Route a single two-qubit gate.
    
    Args:
        gate: (name, (lq1, lq2)) tuple
        mapping: Current {logical: physical} mapping
        edges: Set of connected physical qubit pairs
    Returns:
        (gates_to_insert, new_mapping)
    """
    name, (lq1, lq2) = gate
    pq1, pq2 = mapping[lq1], mapping[lq2]
    
    # Check if adjacent
    if (pq1, pq2) in edges or (pq2, pq1) in edges:
        return [(name, (pq1, pq2))], mapping
    
    # TODO: Find path and insert SWAPs
    pass

# Test
mapping = {0: 0, 1: 2}  # Logical 1 at physical 2
edges = {(0, 1), (1, 2)}  # Linear
print(basic_route(('CX', (0, 1)), mapping, edges))
hints: Find shortest path between physical qubits
Insert SWAP for each step along path except last

testCases.input: CX(0,1) with logical 1 at physical 2 on linear

exercises.title: Routing Score Calculation
exercises.description: Calculate the routing score (total distance) for a potential SWAP.
exercises.starterCode:
def swap_score(pq1, pq2, front_gates, mapping, distances):

    Returns:
        Score (lower is better)

    # TODO: Calculate total distance for front gates
    pass

# Test
mapping = {0: 0, 1: 2}
front = [(0, 1)]  # Need CX between logical 0 and 1
distances = {0: {0: 0, 1: 1, 2: 2}, 1: {0: 1, 1: 0, 2: 1}, 2: {0: 2, 1: 1, 2: 0}}
print(swap_score(0, 1, front, mapping, distances))  # Should improve distance
exercises.solution:
def swap_score(pq1, pq2, front_gates, mapping, distances):
    temp_mapping = dict(mapping)
    reverse = {v: k for k, v in mapping.items()}
    
    # Apply SWAP
    lqa = reverse.get(pq1)
    lqb = reverse.get(pq2)
    if lqa: temp_mapping[lqa] = pq2
    if lqb: temp_mapping[lqb] = pq1
    
    # Calculate total distance
    score = 0
    for lq1, lq2 in front_gates:
        p1 = temp_mapping.get(lq1)
        p2 = temp_mapping.get(lq2)
        if p1 is not None and p2 is not None:
            score += distances.get(p1, {}).get(p2, 10)
    
    return score

mapping = {0: 0, 1: 2}
front = [(0, 1)]
distances = {0: {0: 0, 1: 1, 2: 2}, 1: {0: 1, 1: 0, 2: 1}, 2: {0: 2, 1: 1, 2: 0}}
print(f"No swap: {swap_score(-1, -1, front, mapping, distances)}")
print(f"SWAP(0,1): {swap_score(0, 1, front, mapping, distances)}")
hints: Create a temporary mapping to simulate the SWAP
Sum distances for all front layer gates using the simulated mapping
Lower score means better SWAP choice

testCases.input: SWAP that brings interacting qubits closer
